caseStudy.title: Inventory Intelligence Platform
caseStudy.description: Demand forecasting to eliminate stockouts and overstock.
caseStudy.overview: A consumer electronics retailer was managing inventory reactively, resulting in costly stockouts during peak demand and excessive overstock of slow-moving products. The goal was to build a demand forecasting system to optimize purchasing decisions.
caseStudy.problem: Buyers relied on gut instinct and basic spreadsheet models to forecast demand. Stockouts during product launches cost an estimated $4M in lost sales annually, while overstock required frequent margin-eroding markdowns to clear warehouse space.
caseStudy.solution: A demand forecasting model was trained on 3 years of sales history, incorporating seasonality, marketing calendar, and external signals such as competitor pricing and search trend data. Automated purchase order recommendations were generated weekly and surfaced to buyers for approval via a decision-support dashboard.

results.summary: Buyers now make purchasing decisions backed by data, with confidence intervals that clearly communicate forecast uncertainty. Warehouse utilisation improved, reducing storage costs alongside the margin gains.
